gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/254)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/254)
text: مَنْ شَرِبَ الْمُسْكِرَ وَ مَاتَ وَ فِي جَوْفِهِ مِنْهُ شَيْ‏ءٌ لَمْ يَتُبْ مِنْهُ بُعِثَ مِنْ قَبْرِهِ مُخَبَّلًا مَائِلًا شِدْقُهُ سَائِلًا لُعَابُهُ يَدْعُو بِالْوَيْلِ وَ الثُّبُورِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Ismail Bin Muhammad Al Minqary, from Yazeed Bin Abu Ziyad,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The one who drinks an intoxicant and dies, and in his inside is something from it, not having repented from it, would be Resurrected from his grave confused, his die inclined, his saliva oozing, calling for the woe and the doom’.
en.sarwar: 13. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Hakam from ’Isma‘il ibn Muhammad al-Minqariy from Yazid ibn abu Ziyad who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If one drinks intoxicating liquor and dies when there is something of wine inside him because of which he has not repented, he will be raised from his grave confused, his mouth bent to one side, his saliva flowing and crying woes and destruction.’”
